root_cause_assessment.mv_agents_003: Root cause correctly identified and fixed at the source: POST /agents/test-run lacked the 'llm_model or "deterministic"' fallback that GET /agents/catalog already applies (asymmetry between two endpoints reading the same catalog). Fix renames the raw resolved value to llm_model and derives model = llm_model or "deterministic", using llm_model (not the fallback string) for _price_for() so a deterministic agent's cost estimate correctly stays None rather than erroring on an unpriceable model string. Minimal, surgical diff (7 lines changed in the function body).
root_cause_assessment.mv_agents_004: Root cause correctly identified: _build_provider_entry hard-coded status="connected" the instant a DB credential row existed, never consulting the already-stored lastVerifyStatus the verify endpoints (POST /providers/{p}/verify, POST /user/providers/{p}/verify) write on every real round-trip. Fix reads that stored field and demotes to the pre-existing 'warning' status (already rendered client-side as 'Re-authenticate') only when lastVerifyStatus == 'failed'; 'ok' and never-verified (None) rows keep prior connected-on-row-exists behavior — a deliberately narrow, backward-compatible demotion, not a rewrite of the honesty model. No live re-verify was added on render (confirmed by reading the diff and the function body — only .get("lastVerifyStatus") off the already-fetched masked row).

override_guard_widening_review.change: override.get('status') in ('warning','unconfigured') and status == 'connected' -> ... and status in ('connected','warning')
override_guard_widening_review.analysis: Before this fix, _provider_env_state() only ever returns 'connected'/'unconfigured' and the DB branch was unconditionally 'connected', so the raw pre-override status could never be 'warning' — the guard's 'connected'-only check was exhaustive for all prior states. This fix introduces the first source of a raw 'warning' status (failed-verify DB credential), so the guard had to widen to keep applying the documented 'override may only DOWNGRADE' contract to that new state (unconfigured still beats warning). Verified this does not reach any exercised legit-disconnect path: grep confirmed the frontend (apps/web/src/app/dashboard/agents/page.tsx, apps/web/src/components/agents/api.ts) only ever calls updateProvider() with a {model} patch, never {status} — the PUT /agents/providers/{id} status-override path this guard touches is not wired to any UI disconnect action today. The actual product 'disconnect' flow is DELETE /providers/{provider}/credential, which removes the row outright and does not touch the AgentProvider override table or this guard at all. Full targeted + adjacent regression suites (74 backend tests total across test_agents_screen.py, test_provider_config.py, test_gap_p5_auth_compliance.py, test_gap_p7_def_a_dual_mode.py) pass, so no observed regression.
override_guard_widening_review.conclusion: No break to any real or tested user disconnect flow. Minor residual risk (pre-existing, not introduced by this diff): a stale override row from the legacy status-set path could out-live a later legitimate reconnect — but this exists identically for the 'connected' case pre-fix too, is not exercised by the UI, and is out of scope for MV-agents-004.

minor_observations_non_blocking: Commit message overclaims 'Frontend: 2 new vitest cases' — the actual diff (apps/web/src/__tests__/agents/agents-screen.test.ts) adds exactly 1 new `it(...)` block, matching the task brief's own singular 'the FE Zod test'. Verified by grepping the diff for added it()/test() lines. Not a functional defect, but a testimony/evidence mismatch worth flagging per epistemic-discipline rules.

TestRunModal.tsx's 'actual run' sentence (responseSeconds/actualCost/actualTokens) requires ALL THREE non-null to render the real-figures sentence, else falls back to the honest 'No completed run yet' message — so a hypothetical partial-null backend payload (e.g. duration_ms missing but costUsd present) would suppress genuinely-available data rather than show it. Conservative (never fabricates, never crashes) so not a defect, just a slight precision loss; current backend code always sets the three together from one `out` object so this is not reachable today.
